title: Priority Constituencies by State
description: Write a query joining all three tables (constituency_info, election_results, voter_turnout) to find constituencies where winning_margin < 50000 AND turnout_percent < 65. Then group by state and count them as priority_constituencies.

schema: CREATE TABLE constituency_info (constituency_id INTEGER, constituency TEXT, state TEXT)
CREATE TABLE election_results (constituency_id INTEGER, candidate TEXT, party TEXT, total_votes INTEGER, winning_margin INTEGER)
CREATE TABLE voter_turnout (constituency_id INTEGER, turnout_percent REAL)
testCases.solutionQuery: SELECT c.state, COUNT(*) AS priority_constituencies FROM constituency_info c INNER JOIN election_results e ON c.constituency_id = e.constituency_id INNER JOIN voter_turnout t ON c.constituency_id = t.constituency_id WHERE e.winning_margin < 50000 AND t.turnout_percent < 65 GROUP BY c.state

schema: CREATE TABLE constituency_info (constituency_id INTEGER, constituency TEXT, state TEXT)
CREATE TABLE election_results (constituency_id INTEGER, candidate TEXT, party TEXT, total_votes INTEGER, winning_margin INTEGER)
CREATE TABLE voter_turnout (constituency_id INTEGER, turnout_percent REAL)
testCases.solutionQuery: SELECT c.state, COUNT(*) AS priority_constituencies FROM constituency_info c INNER JOIN election_results e ON c.constituency_id = e.constituency_id INNER JOIN voter_turnout t ON c.constituency_id = t.constituency_id WHERE e.winning_margin < 50000 AND t.turnout_percent < 65 GROUP BY c.state
